title: Dialogue with Iranians: LaRouche Oasis Plan Is Pathway to Peace
description:
Peace between Palestine and Israel? Really? Yes, by conceptualizing a higher outlook which encompasses the best for both. Adopting the LaRouche Oasis Plan for economic development, especially for water and transport, will do just that. On May 23, Schiller Institute Science Advisor Jason Ross engaged with the Organization for Defending Victims of Violence (ODVV), an Iranian NGO, on the LaRouche Oasis Plan. A lively Q&A session followed Ross’s tour d’horizon of the plan.

Topics covered:
* Lyndon LaRouche’s idea of the uniqueness of each human mind to solve problems
* deadliest enemy in the region is lack of access to water
* the British Empire’s historical role in conflict
* the role of the BRICS in working for regional projects
* the U.S. candidacies of Diane Sare and Jose Vega as examples of Americans standing up for justice.

One ODVV participant, who found the Oasis Plan to be “inclusive and comprehensive,” asked what obstacles, besides the U.S., stand in its way.

Ross noted three:
1) the historical role of the British in manipulating conflicts in the region as a means of control, to prevent land connectivity in this crossroads of the world
2) Western countries’ resistance to infrastructure and growth in general
3) the notion of “scarcity,” or the environmentalist rejection of the creativity of the human mind creating new resources.

Thinking “Big” for a future, this is the way to overcome prevailing cynicism.

In closing, the ODVV research director said, “It is a promising plan and all countries will benefit from it. It will create more access to resources for everybody. With it we can say ‘good-bye’ to the era of war, killing, genocide, discrimination … and scarcity. If we can change minds, we can adopt a new approach.”
